photojournalism.newsExtreme.title: News extreme
photojournalism.newsExtreme.description: Catastrophic, world-altering, and extreme global news events and disasters
examples: a global agricultural blight rapidly destroys half of the world's wheat and corn crops
a major city is hit by a massive blizzard, shutting down transportation and power
a major corporation is caught in a massive bribery scandal
a massive earthquake strikes a major metropolitan area
a massive avalanche buries a famous alpine ski resort, trapping hundreds of tourists
a major historical artifact is discovered, rewriting history as we know it

photojournalism.newsNormal.title: News normal
photojournalism.newsNormal.description: Standard major news headlines covering science, politics, business, and society
examples: a major geopolitical event, such as a peace treaty or a declaration of war
a major political scandal, involving a high-ranking official in a major world government
a natural phenomenon, such as a solar eclipse or meteor shower, that captures the world's attention
a major breakthrough in space exploration, such as the discovery of a new planet or the launch of a new mission to the moon
a major environmental crisis, such as deforestation or coral reef destruction
a major world crisis, such as the covid-19 pandemic or a global economic recession

photojournalism.newsSpecific.title: News specific
photojournalism.newsSpecific.description: Highly detailed, specific descriptions of major global news events and crises
examples: a major political scandal: a top government official is caught in a sexting scandal, leading to calls for his resignation and damaging his political career
a dramatic urban rescue: firefighters work tirelessly to rescue residents trapped on the upper floors of a burning skyscraper in downtown chicago
a historic peace accord: two neighboring countries sign a landmark treaty ending decades of border conflict
a technological breakthrough: scientists create a new type of battery that is both more efficient and less expensive than existing technologies, revolutionizing the energy industry
a major environmental crisis: a massive oil spill off the coast of brazil threatens wildlife and fishing communities, leading to calls for greater regulation of the oil industry
a major cybersecurity breach: a state-sponsored hacking group accesses sensitive personnel records of millions of federal employees

photojournalism.styleProtest.title: Style protest
photojournalism.styleProtest.description: Various photographic styles and techniques used in photojournalism and documentary photography
examples: intentional camera movement: deliberately moving the camera during a longer exposure to create a dynamic, abstract representation of chaos and action
creative lighting photography: using creative lighting techniques to add drama and impact to protest images
double exposure photography: combining two distinct images, such as a protester's face and a crowded street, to create a layered, symbolic narrative
photojournalistic essay: using a series of photographs to tell a story about the protests and the issues they are addressing
black and white photography: creating a timeless and classic aesthetic that emphasizes contrast and emotion
tintype and wet plate photography: employing historic nineteenth-century processes to produce highly textured, hauntingly historicized portraits

photojournalism.types.title: Types of photojournalism
photojournalism.types.description: Diverse subjects and thematic beats covered in photojournalism and documentary reporting
examples: coverage of labor and workers' rights protests and strikes
coverage of human rights abuses and violations
investigative reporting on government corruption and scandals

plot.action.title: Action plots

defending against a home invasion or siege
escaping from a dangerous maze or labyrinth
rescuing a captive from a jungle or wilderness
intercepting a deadly smuggler shipment at sea

experiencing a surreal breakdown of linear time

set.moviegenBench.title: Benchmark prompt set from MovieGenBench
set.moviegenBench.description: MovieGen Video Bench contains 1003 diverse prompts testing various aspects including human activities (motion, emotions), animals, nature/scenery, physics (fluids, gravity, collisions), and unusual subjects/activities. The prompts provide comprehensive coverage across high, medium and low motion scenarios.
examples: A high-speed video of raindrops hitting a puddle, causing ripples and splashes.

A stylish woman walks down a Tokyo street filled with warm glowing neon and animated city signage. She wears a black leather jacket, a long red dress, and black boots, and carries a black purse. She wears sunglasses and red lipstick. She walks confidently and casually. The street is damp and reflective, creating a mirror effect of the colorful lights. Many pedestrians walk about.
A tsunami coming through an alley in Bulgaria, dynamic movement.
Tears of joy streamed down a woman's face as she reunited with a long-lost friend.

A dynamic motion shot of a plant transforming into an octopus. The green leaves of the plant begin to elongate and twist, turning into flexible, writhing tentacles. The camera circles as the stem thickens and expands, morphing into the bulbous head of an octopus, its texture shifting to a mottled pattern of green. The transformation completes with the plant revealing a fully formed octopus, its tentacles moving gracefully in the water.
An astronaut walking between stone buildings.

set.parti.title: PartiPrompts

a dump truck filled with soccer balls scuba diving in a coral reef.
